content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6446

March 28, 1979                                    A-79-OSC

Philip M. Madden
Kilpatrick, Clayton, Mayer
and Madden
200 Pine Avenue, Suite 606
P.O. Box 2210
Long Beach, CA  90801

Dear Mr. Madden:

     This is in response to the letter dated March 16, 1979,
from Douglas Otto, previously of your office, confirming our
telephone conversation of that date. I have two modifications
to the statements in Mr. Otto's letter.

     The late contribution reporting requirement, Government
Code Section 84214, imposes a 48 hour reporting deadline on
late contributions. For makers of late contributions that
are committees, the late contribution must be reported
within 48 hours of the making to the appropriate filing
officers. Therefore North American Phillips Lighting Cor-
poration in Highstown, New Jersey was required to file its
report within 48 hours of its making the $35,000 contribution.
The No IV Committee ("Committee") was required to file
its late contribution report within 48 hours of its receipt
of the $35,000 contribution. Your letter incorrectly suggests
that North American Phillips Lighting Corporation and the
Committee would be required to file at the same time.

     With regard to the post-election filing requirements, I
do not recall advising that the Committee's "next and final
filing obligation" would be 65 days subsequent to the March 20,
1979, election. The campaign statement filed 65 days after
the election will complete the Committee's election filings
for the special election. However, if there is subsequent
activity (contributions received or expenditures made) at a
later time, semiannual or other election campaign statements
will have to be filed. Government Code Sections 84200-04,
84206. The Committee will only cease to have a filing obli-
gation when it terminates as a committee. See 2 Cal. Adm.

Philip M. Madden
March 28, 1979
Page Two


Code Section 18404, a copy of which is enclosed, which out-
lines the conditions under which a recipient committee can
terminate.

I hope these modifications are clear to you. If you
have any other questions, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

BC:plh

Enclosure

KILPATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MADDEN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

# J. KILPATRICK
STERLING S. CLAYTON
DONALD W. MEYER
PHILIP M. MADDEN
OKRIN S. P. GAUMAN
JUDY J. JOHNSON

200 PINE AVENUE SUITE 606
POST OFFICE BOX 283
LONG BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90801
(213) 435-6565
(213) 775-3208

March 16, 1979

Barbara Campbell
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Campbell:

It was a pleasure to speak to you today concerning campaign disclosure requirements for the No On IV Committee for the forthcoming March 20, 1979 Special Municipal Election to be held in Long Beach, California. You provided me with much useful information and alleviated most of my anxiety with regard to late contribution filings pursuant to California Government Code §84214 and the post-election disclosure filing required of the Committee under §84200 of the same code.

The purpose of this letter is to confirm the conclusions we reached today. I informed you that the No On IV Committee today received a $35,000 contribution from North American Philips Lighting Corporation of Highstown, New Jersey. The same amount was immediately deposited in the Committee's account in the Farmers & Merchants Bank at 302 Pine Avenue, Long Beach. You, in turn, informed me that the occasion of this contribution required a "late contribution" filing by both the Recipient Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation within 48 hours. Since the appropriate filing location for proponents and opponents of local ballot measures is the office of the City Clerk and that office is closed on Sunday, March 18, 1979, when the 48 hour time period expires, you informed me that a Monday filing, March 19, 1979, would satisfy the requirements of Government Code §84214.

Later, in the same conversation, we discussed post-election filing requirements for both the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation. First you told me that the Committee's next and final filing obligation would be 65 days subsequent to the March 20, 1979 election and that the appropriate form for filing was Form 420. You then informed

Barbara Campbell
March 16, 1979
Page Two

me that since North American Philips Lighting Corporation con-
tributed more than $5,000 within a calendar year to political
endeavors, it was required to file a Major Donor form, Form 461,
including all contributions and independent expenditures made
for political purposes.

Both the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting
Corporation are relying on the above advice. Should any of the
statements contained in the preceding paragraph not coincide with
your recollection of our conversation, please inform me at the
earliest possible moment either by mail or by telephone.

Prior to our conversation, I spoke with Gene Morrow in the
Secretary of State's Office and he provided me with the same
information on which you and I have agreed. Once again, I want
to thank you for your assistance.

Yours very truly,

KILPATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MADDEN

[signature]

Douglas W. Otto

DWOsjo

KILPATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MADDEN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

R. J. KILPATRICK
STERLING S. CLAYTON
DONALD W. MEYER
PHILIP M. MADDEN
DENNIS P. GAUDAN
JUDY J. JOHNSON

200 PINE AVENUE, SUITE 606
POST OFFICE BOX 280
LONG BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90801
(213) 435-6565
(213) 776-3206

March 16, 1979


Barbara Campbell
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Campbell:

It was a pleasure to speak to you today concerning campaign
disclosure requirements for the No On IV Committee for the
forthcoming March 20, 1979 Special Municipal Election to be
held in Long Beach, California. You provided me with much
useful information and alleviated most of my anxiety with
regard to late contribution filings pursuant to California
Government Code §84214 and the post-election disclosure filing
required of the Committee under §84200 of the same code.

The purpose of this letter is to confirm the conclusions we
reached today. I informed you that the No On IV Committee
today received a $35,000 contribution from North American
Philips Lighting Corporation of Highstown, New Jersey. The
same amount was immediately deposited in the Committee's
account in the Farmers & Merchants Bank at 302 Pine Avenue,
Long Beach, CA. You, in turn, informed me that the occasion of
this contribution required a "late contribution" filing by
both the Recipient Committee and North American Philips
Lighting Corporation within 48 hours. Since the appropriate
filing location for proponents and opponents of local ballot
measures is the office of the City Clerk and that office is
closed on Sunday, March 18, 1979, when the 48 hour time period
expires, you informed that a Monday filing, March 19, 1979,
would satisfy the requirements of Government Code §84214.

Later, in the same conversation, we discussed post-election
filing requirements for both the No On IV Committee and North
American Philips Lighting Corporation. First you told me that
the Committee's next and final filing obligation would be 65
days subsequent to the March 20, 1979 election and that the
appropriate form for filing was Form 420. You then informed

Barbara Campbell
March 16, 1979
Page Two

me that since North American Philips Lighting Corporation con-
tributed more than $5,000 within a calendar year to political
endeavors, it was required to file a Major Donor form, Form 461,
including all contributions and independent expenditures made
for political purposes.

Both the No on IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting
Corporation are relying on the above advice. Should any of the
statements contained in the preceding paragraph not coincide with
your recollection of our conversation, please inform me at the
earliest possible moment either by mail or by telephone.

Prior to our conversation, I spoke with Gene Morrow in the
Secretary of State's Office and he provided me with the same
information on which you and I have agreed. Once again, I want
to thank you for your assistance.

Yours very truly,

KILPATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MADDEN

Douglas W. Otto

DWOsjc

sections.question_synthetic: What are the late contribution reporting requirements and post-election filing obligations for the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation regarding a $35,000 contribution made before a March 20, 1979 special election in Long Beach?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarifies that North American Phillips Lighting Corporation was required to file a late contribution report within 48 hours of making the $35,000 contribution, and the No On IV Committee was similarly required to file its late contribution report within 48 hours. Additionally, the FPPC modifies previous advice regarding post-election filing obligations and provides guidance on committee termination under Government Code Section 18404.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (March 16, 1979 from Douglas W. Otto) and FPPC response letter (March 28, 1979 from Barbara Campbell). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers; information extracted from letter body. Some OCR degradation present in source document affecting readability. Focus of extraction is the FPPC response portion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the late contribution reporting requirements and post-election filing obligations for the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation regarding a $35,000 contribution made before a March 20, 1979 special election in Long Beach?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC clarifies that North American Phillips Lighting Corporation was required to file a late contribution report within 48 hours of making the $35,000 contribution, and the No On IV Committee was similarly required to file its late contribution report within 48 hours. Additionally, the FPPC modifies previous advice regarding post-election filing obligations and provides guidance on committee termination under Government Code Section 18404.
